name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Aligning down should never produce a value greater than the original input

name: align_not_idempotent
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_not_idempotent(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a % b == 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: Aligning an already-aligned value should be the identity; changing it would be unsound

name: align_result_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_3_align_result_not_aligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) % b != 0,
{
}
reason: The whole purpose of align_to_usize is to produce a b-aligned result; failing that breaks all downstream alignment invariants

name: zero_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_4_zero_not_aligned(size: nat)
    requires
        size > 0,
    ensures
        !aligned(0 as nat, size),
{
}
reason: Zero is divisible by every nonzero number, so aligned(0, size) must be true for all size > 0

name: aligned_disagrees_with_align
code:
proof fn phi_5_aligned_disagrees_with_align(addr: usize, size: usize)
    requires
        size > 0,
        aligned(addr as nat, size as nat),
    ensures
        align_to_usize(addr, size) != addr,
{
}
reason: If aligned says addr is aligned then align_to_usize must be the identity on it; disagreement means the two specs are inconsistent
